title: Military parade
diff_added:
The national parades are held in a host city selected for the year, and typically include military unis from all three services, the [[Finnish Border Guard]] and [[Territorial Forces (Finland)|reserve units]], contingents from  the [[National Defence Training Association of Finland]] and other voluntary national defence organisations, and contingents of civilian authorities, e.g. the [[Police of Finland]]. Helsinki hosts the June 4 flag day parade every fifth year; 1997, 2002, 2007, 2012, 2017, 2022, 2027, and so on.
The [[Pakistan Day Parade]], also known as the National Day Joint Services Parade, is an annual event held at [[Shakarparian]] in [[Islamabad]] to commemorate the [[Pakistan Day]] on 23 March, marking the anniversary of the 1940 [[Lahore Resolution]]. The parade is presided over by the [[President of Pakistan]] and the [[Prime Minister of Pakistan]], alongside the [[Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff Committee]], and the Chiefs of the [[Chief of Army Staff (Pakistan)|Army]], [[Chief of Naval Staff (Pakistan)|Navy]], and [[Chief of Air Staff (Pakistan)|Air Force]]. Foreign dignitaries often attend as special guests. The event is organised by [[Joint Staff Headquarters (Pakistan)|Joint Staff Headquarters]] and showcases the country's military strength and national unity.
The first known organized military procession in the [[United States]] was the [[Grand Review of the Armies]], on May 23 and May 24, 1865, following the end of the [[American Civil War]] (1861–1865). The [[New York City Victory Parade of 1946]] was held in mid-January in 1946 to commemorate the conclusion of [[World War II]] and the Allied victory over the Axis Powers in all theaters of the war, helped by the service of millions of Americans who served under the armed forces and the National Guard, in addition to the state defense forces. In the late 40s and the early 50s, massive parades in honor of Army Day and later Armed Forces Day were held in the capital.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://networks.h-net.org/node/28441/discussions/1341907/washington-loves-parade|title=Washington Loves a Parade | H-DC | H-Net|website=networks.h-net.org|access-date=Aug 25, 2020}}</ref> The [[National Victory Celebration]] parade was held on June 8, 1991, to celebrate the conclusion of [[Gulf War]] in [[Iraq]]. The [[US Army 250th Anniversary Parade]] was held on June 14, 2025, to celebrate the Army's 250th birthday.

comment: Kerala's new official name is keralam

title: Age of Empires III: The Asian Dynasties
comment: /* Campaigns */ clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: Company → company (3)
diff_added: }}</ref> Nanib uses one of the rifles to fire a near miss at Edwardson (loosely based on a similar incident involving [[Mangal Pandey]]), killing one of his soldiers. Nanib and fellow sepoy Pravar Patel then lead their regiment of sepoy in an attack on the local Company fort by assaulting weapon caches to cause fires and explosions under its foundations. After their victory, Nanib and Pravar quickly rally the local citizens and sepoys, raising enormous forces. Although Nanib denies leadership of the revolt, he and Pravar decide to rescue [[Bahadur Shah II]] from British captivity when the Shah declares himself supreme ruler of India. They sneak into Delhi in the dead of night and destroy weapon caches to cause elephant stampedes, which destroy various city gates. Along the way, they find more rebels who help them battle through [[Delhi]], freeing the Shah and escaping into the night. Nanib subsequently leads his forces in an assault on a Company fort in [[Shimla]] commanded by Colonel Edwardson. Nanib destroys or captures the saltpetre sites and plantations supplying Edwardson's forces inside the fort and fends off three counterattacks. Finally, he assaults the fort and captures its outer defenses, including the fixed guns, mortally wounding Edwardson in the process before destroying the fort's command post. When the heat of battle fades, Nanib approaches a dying Edwardson, who threatens and curses the Indians, saying that the company is a much larger and more powerful force than all of India. Nanib replies by explaining that no military force can kill the Indians' passion for their country. After Edwardson dies, Nanib and Pravar leave to prepare for continued war with the company, and three rebel sepoys are seen lowering the company's flag from the nearby flagpost.

title: Eastern Canada Professional Soccer League
diff_added:
The 1961 season featured a 48-match schedule from 14 May to 20 September, with all four teams playing 24 matches each. Matches were played at [[Varsity Stadium]] in Toronto, [[Ivor Wynne Stadium|Civic Centre]] in Hamilton, and [[Delorimier Stadium|Stade Delorimier]] in Montréal. Hamilton Steelers won the league's opening match 2–1, although it was Montréal Cantalia's [[Ismael Ferreyra]] who scored the league's first goal in the 14 May opener.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.google.com/newspapers?nid=Fr8DH2VBP9sC&dat=19610515&printsec=frontpage&hl=en|title=The Montreal Gazette - Google News Archive Search|website=news.google.com}}</ref> An all-star match was played on 4 September featuring stars from Toronto's two teams against stars from Hamilton-Montréal. The regular season was then followed by two rounds of playoffs from 21 September to 9 October.
* [[Hamilton Steelers (ECPSL)|Hamilton Steelers]] (1961–1964) / Primo Hamilton FC (1965–1966)
* Montréal Italica (1964–1965) / Inter Italica (1966)
* [[Toronto City|Toronto City FC]] (1961–1965)
* Toronto Roma FC (1962-1964 / Inter-Roma (1965–1966)
* [[Toronto Falcons (1967–1968)|Toronto Italia FC]] (1961–1964) / Italia Falcons (1965–66)

title: Kobra and the Lotus
comment: clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: the USA → the US (2), full length → full-length, 2012-2013 → 2012–2013
diff_added:
The band recorded their second album self-titled ''Kobra and the Lotus'' February to April 2011, with producer [[Julius Butty]] then headed to Europe for a UK summer tour.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bravewords.com/news/163792 |title=KOBRA AND THE LOTUS Kick Off UK Tour from Ibiza's Hard Rock Hell Festival |website=Bravewords.com |access-date=January 17, 2012}}</ref> While touring, the band's manager Susan Bullen had been in touch with several labels regarding the second album and reached out to [[Mark Spicoluk]] at [[Universal Music Group]] and played some preliminary tracks from the new album. Universal Canada thought the album needed an anthem and encouraged the band to write four new tracks and go back into the studio January and February 2012 with fellow Canadian [[Kevin Churko]] to record the new tracks and remix and remaster the entire album. The songs "50 Shades of Evil"<ref>{{cite web|url=http://kobraandthelotus.com/blog-3-one-song-one-story-50-shades-evil/|title=50 Shades of Evil: One Song, One Story|website=kobraandthelotus.com|access-date=March 28, 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170328200609/http://kobraandthelotus.com/blog-3-one-song-one-story-50-shades-evil/|archive-date=March 28, 2017|url-status=dead}}</ref> and "Forever One" were two of the new tracks written. The self-titled album was globally released in August 2012 in the UK and Europe by [[Spinefarm Records]] and in 138 other countries by [[Universal Music Group]] affiliates. It was then released in the US and Canada in April 2013 with Universal Music Enterprises UMe and T-Boy Records. The album rated as 7/8 POWERPOINTS by PowerPlay (music magazine) in issue #145,<ref>{{cite web |url=http://powerplaymagazine.co.uk/issue-145/ |title=Issue 145 - Powerplay Rock and Metal Magazine |website=Powerplaymagazine.co.uk |access-date=2016-08-03 |archive-date=August 25, 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170825233024/http://powerplaymagazine.co.uk/issue-145/ |url-status=dead }}</ref> rated as KKKK by ''[[Kerrang!]]'', and 8/10 by ''[[Metal Hammer]]''.
The band recorded 14 new tracks for their new album ''High Priestess''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://musicenthusiastmag.com/kobra-and-the-lotus-high-priestess-album-review/#ixzz35NzVurkc |title=Kobra and the Lotus, 'High Priestess' - Album Review |website=Musicenthusiastmag.com |access-date=2016-08-03}}</ref> with super producer [[Johnny K]], a Grammy Award nominated producer/engineer/mixer/musician and songwriter at Groovemaster Recording Studios in Chicago. [[Charlie Parra del Riego]] joined as special guest guitarist for the recording. ''High Priestess'' entered the CMJ Loud Rock Radio charts in the US at #4,  it was released globally on June 24, 2014.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.musictrespass.com/content/kobra-and-lotus-release-new-album-high-priestess |title=Kobra and the Lotus - Release new album 'High Priestess' !!! |work=Music Trespass |date=2014-09-29 |access-date=2016-08-03}}</ref> to strong reviews worl

'''Alinka Echeverría''' (born 1981) is a Mexican-British  visual anthropologist, artist, filmmaker, and broadcaster.<ref name="auto1">{{Cite web |title=Biography |url=http://www.alinkaecheverria.com/education |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=Alinka Echeverria}}</ref>
Alinka Echeverría was born in 1981 in [[Mexico City, Mexico]]. She was trained as an anthropologist and earned her master's degree in Social Anthropology from [[University of Edinburgh|the University of Edinburgh]] in 2004, with an exchange year at the [[University of Bologna]] in Italy. Echeverría then worked on HIV prevention NGO projects in East Africa for several years.<ref name="auto">{{Cite web |date=2019-04-01 |title=Reimagining the Image: Alinka Echeverría |url=https://www.icp.org/browse/archive/media/reimagining-the-image-alinka-echeverria |access-date=2022-03-18 |website=International Center of Photography}}</ref> She studied photography at the [[International Center of Photography|International Center for Photography]] in New York and graduated with a postgraduate degree in 2008.<ref name="auto1"/> From 2010 to 2011, she was artist-in-residence at L'École Nationale Supérieure de la Photographie in Arles, France.

title: Vladimir Dubrovskii
comment: clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: Deputy director → deputy director, Editorial board → editorial board (3), 2008-2014 → 2008–2014 (3)
diff_added:
He is best known for growth modeling of semiconductor nanostructures, particularly III-V nanowires.  Starting from 2003, he has been at the forefront of research in this field, collaborating with more than 40 groups in 18 countries (with joint publications). His main area is in kinetically controlled engineering of nanostructures, including morphology, crustal phase, and size distributions. In 2005, he and coauthors proved a diffusion-induced character of gold-assisted vapor-liquid-solid (VLS) growth of GaAs nanowires by [[molecular beam]] epitaxy [1]. In 2008–2014, following [[Frank Glas]] [2], he developed theoretical approaches for understanding and controlling polytypism of III-V nanowires by the growth parameter tuning [3] and catalyst material [4]. This allowed achieving record small GaAs nanowires (down to 5 nm in radius) with pure zincblende structure [5]. Independently of Jerry Tersoff [6], in 2013-2015 he predicted a non-linear focusing effect [7,8] that enabled self-organized ensembles of GaAs nanowires with uniform radii [8]. The works of 2016 brought up the new size distributions describing length statistics in nanowire ensembles [9,10]. In 2015–2016, he developed the first theory for the compositional control of ternary III-V nanowires [11], sharpening their axial heterointerfaces [12] and, more generally, nucleation theory of ternary solids from ternary and quaternary liquid alloys. He contributed into understanding the VLS versus selective area growth of nanowires [13] and self-induced nucleation of GaN nanowires on silicon substrates [14].
In 2009, Dubrovskii discovered fluctuation-induced broadening (the Dubrovskii broadening) of the size distributions described by a Fokker-Planck type kinetic equation in terms of the Kuni invariant variables [15], and presented a map of the power exponents for the spectrum spreading in 2D and 3D systems. Further studies revealed the influence of kinetic fluctuations on the size distributions of islands and droplets in the stages of their nucleation, growth, and [[Ostwald ripening]] [16,17]. He also contributed into binary nucleation theory with a saddle point of the formation energy, with applications in growth theory of strain-induced islands [18] and ternary VLS nanowires.
In 1996, he published exact solution for the infinite set of rate equations for heterogeneous growth with size-linear rate constants [19], reduced to one-parametric Polya distribution. Further investigation of the growth systems with size-linear capture rates led to a two-parametric modified beta-distribution (2015) [20] which acquires the Vicsek-Family scaling form [21] in the [[continuum limit]]. Distributions of this type are now widely used for modeling the growth kinetics of semiconductor nanostructures, surface islands and biological objects.

title: Ponderomotive force
comment: clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: For example → For example,, english → English
diff_added:
The term ''ponderomotive'' comes from the Latin ''ponder-'' (meaning weight) and the English ''motive'' (having to do with motion).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/ponderomotive|access-date=2023-09-27|title=ponderomotive}}</ref>
where <math>\boldsymbol{\beta}=\boldsymbol{v}/c</math>, <math>\bar{\gamma}</math> is the time-averaged particle energy in the units of the rest mass, and <math>\omega - \boldsymbol{k}\cdot\boldsymbol{v}</math> can be interpreted as a Doppler-shifted angular frequency. Note that <math>k</math> and <math>\omega</math> do not have to follow any particular [[dispersion relation]]. For example, in a [[Undulator|magnetic undulator]], <math>\omega</math> is zero, and in a [[standing wave]] <math>\boldsymbol{k}</math> is 0, as the spatial variation must be included in the envelope of <math>\hat \boldsymbol{f}</math>.

title: Leonard Owen
comment: /* Biography */ clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: Managing Director → managing director, Chairman → chairman
diff_added:
With the establishment of the [[United Kingdom Atomic Energy Authority]] (UKAEA) in 1954, Owen became the Director of Engineering and Deputy Managing Director of its Industrial Group. When Hinton moved on to become the chairman of the [[Central Electricity Generating Board]] in 1957, Owen became the managing director.<ref name="ice"/> He became the UKAEA Member for Production in 1959, and Member for Production and for Engineering in 1961.<ref name="na"/> He served in this role until he retired from executive duties in 1962, but remained as a part-time member of the UKAEA until 1964.<ref name="ice"/> He was made a [[Knight Bachelor]] in 1957,<ref>{{London Gazette |date=15 February 1957 |issue=41003 |page=1044}}</ref> and awarded an honorary [[Doctor of Science]] degree by the [[University of Manchester]] in 1960.<ref name="na"/>
After leaving the UKAEA, Owen became a Director of United Gas Industries, and was chairman of three of its subsidiaries. He was also a Director of [[Cammell Laird]],<ref name="ice" /> a shipbuilding company that built nuclear-powered submarines, including the [[UK Polaris programme|Polaris]] [[ballistic missile submarine]]s {{HMS|Renown|S26|6}} and {{HMS|Revenge|S27|6}}, and {{HMS|Conqueror|S48|6}}, which sank the cruiser {{ship|ARA|General Belgrano||2}} in the [[Falklands War]].<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/cammell-laird-closure-to-end-mersey-shipbuilding-1561143.html |access-date=20 July 2018 |newspaper=The Independent |title=Cammell Laird closure to end Mersey shipbuilding |first=Michael |last=Harrison |date=3 December 1992}}</ref> He was on the Council of the University of Liverpool, and was chairman of the committee responsible building the new Mechanical Engineering Building and the Oliver Lodge Laboratory. He was chairman of the [[British Nuclear Energy Society]] and a member of the [[Institution of Civil Engineers]], serving on its Publications Committee and its General Purposes Committee.<ref name="ice"/>

title: Küllike Jürimäe
comment: /* Career */ clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: Master's degree → master's degree, six year → six-year
diff_added:
Following her graduation, Jürimäe entered the public administration and became an assistant to the Public Prosecutor of [[Tallinn]] from 1986 until 1991.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Entry into office of new Members at the Court of Justice and the General Court - EU monitor|url=https://www.eumonitor.eu/9353000/1/j9vvik7m1c3gyxp/vje5cb3leqz1?ctx=vg9obtulslrn&start_tab0=20|access-date=2021-01-21|website=www.eumonitor.eu}}</ref> From 1991 onwards she followed up on her studies at the Estonian school of diplomacy from where she obtained her diploma in 1992.<ref name=":0" />   Besides, she was a legal adviser from 1991 and a general counsellor from 1992, both at the Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Estland. She became a Judge at the Court of Appeal of Tallinn in 1993, a post she held until 2004.<ref>{{Cite web|title=The President of the Republic of Estonia 2001-2006: The President of the Republic appointed judges|url=https://vp2001-2006.president.ee/en/duties/press_releases.php?gid=47946|access-date=2021-01-21|website=vp2001-2006.president.ee}}</ref> Between 2002 and 2003 she followed up on her studies at the [[University of Padua|Universities of Padua]] and [[University of Nottingham|Nottingham]] and graduated with a European master's degree in Democratization and Human Rights.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2017-08-14|title=Küllike Jürimäe|url=https://www.just.ee/en/kullike-jurimae|access-date=2021-01-21|website=Ministry of Justice|language=en|archive-date=2021-01-30|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210130065432/https://www.just.ee/en/kullike-jurimae|url-status=dead}}</ref>
On 12 May 2004 Jürimäe became a Judge at the [[General Court (European Union)|General Court of the European Union]],<ref>{{Cite web|title=Küllike Jurimae {{!}} Association du Master Droit et Contentieux de l'Union européenne - Université Panthéon-Assas|url=http://mdcue.u-paris2.fr/parrains/kullike-jurimae/|access-date=2021-01-21|language=fr-FR}}</ref> from where she resigned on 23 October 2013.<ref>{{Cite web|last=|first=|date=9 October 2013|title=Gerichtshof der Europäischen Union, Rücktritt von Frau Küllike Jürimäe|url=https://www.parlament.gv.at/PAKT/EU/XXIV/EU/12/95/EU_129500/imfname_10420626.pdf|archive-url=|archive-date=|access-date=21 January 2021|website=www.parlament.gv.at}}</ref> On 1 April 2015, she was one of the six newly nominated judges at the [[European Court of Justice]] in [[Luxembourg City|Luxembourg]] for a six-year term beginning on 7 October 2015.<ref>{{Cite web|title=6 New Judges Sworn in to European Court of Justice in Luxembourg|url=https://www.chronicle.lu/category/politics/13570-6-new-judges-sworn-in-to-european-court-of-justice-in-luxembourg|access-date=2021-01-21|website=Chronicle.lu}}</ref> In 2021, she was re-appointed for another term until 2027.<ref>Mia Bartoloni (9 July 2021), [https://www.theparliamentmagazine.eu/news/article/movers-and-shakers-9-july-2021 Movers & Shakers] ''[[The Parliament Magazine]]''.</ref>

title: MMA - Meu Melhor Amigo
diff_added:
'''''MMA - Meu Melhor Amigo''''' is a 2025 Brazilian [[Sports film|sports]] [[Comedy drama|comedy-drama film]] directed by José Alvarenga Júnior and starring [[Marcos Mion]], [[Antônio Fagundes]], and Guilherme Tavares. The screenplay was written by Paulo Cursino and Marcos Mion himself, and it was produced by Formata Produções e Conteúdo in partnership with [[The Walt Disney Company Latin America|Star Original Productions]] and [[Globo Filmes]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jr |first=Francisco Paiva |date=2025-01-16 |title=Em entrevista, Mion fala do seu filme, de autismo e de conexões familiares |url=https://www.canalautismo.com.br/noticia/em-entrevista-mion-fala-do-seu-filme-de-autismo-e-de-conexoes-familiares/ |access-date=2025-08-30 |website=Canal Autismo}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite web |last=Nobrega |first=Bruna |date=2024-08-22 |title=Marcos Mion revela trailer de MMA - Meu Melhor Amigo: "Aqui, não sou o Mionzera" |url=https://www.omelete.com.br/filmes/mma-meu-melhor-amigo-trailer-exclusivo-entrevista-marcos-mion |access-date=2025-08-30 |website=Omelete |language=pt-br}}</ref>

In collaboration with director José Alvarenga, Mion aimed to develop a character distinct from his public persona. The team continuously refined scenes and dialogue to ensure Max's authenticity. Initially conceived as a current champion, the character was later changed to a former champion who had been out of the ring for five years, a decision that aligned with Mion's age and appearance. A shoulder injury, which limits Max's fighting ability, was also added to the script. This plot point was included to reflect Mion's own physical limitations, reinforcing the film's theme of personal and professional struggle.<ref name=":0" />
Its premiere at the [[Rio de Janeiro International Film Festival|Festival do Rio]] took place on October 11, 2024, and it was released in cinemas in Brazil on January 16, 2025.<ref>{{Cite web |last=indopraorlando |date=2024-10-03 |title=“MMA - Meu Melhor Amigo”, Protagonizado por Marcos Mion, Tem Estreia no Festival do Rio 2024 |url=https://www.indopraorlando.com.br/mma-meu-melhor-amigo-protagonizado-por-marcos-mion-tem-estreia-no-festival-do-rio-2024/ |access-date=2025-08-30 |website=Indo Pra Orlando |language=pt-BR}}</ref><ref name=":5" />
''MMA - Meu Melhor Amigo'' had a positive reception at the box office. According to [[Comscore]], during its opening weekend from January 16–19, 2024, the film debuted in 10th place among the most-watched films in Brazilian cinemas. It drew an audience of approximately 45,740 people, generating over R$1 million in revenue.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-01-20 |title=“Ainda Estou Aqui” ultrapassa 3,5 milhões de espectadores nos cinemas brasileiros |url=https://pipocamoderna.com.br/2025/01/ainda-estou-aqui-ultrapassa-35-milhoes-de-espectadores-nos-cinemas-brasileiros/ |access-date=2025-08-30 |website=Pipoca Moderna |language=pt-BR}}</ref> Over its 35-day commercial run, the film accumulated a total audience of 89,605 p

title: Luis Carrión Beltrán
diff_added:
{{Undisclosed paid|date=May 2020}}
Back in Mexico in 1970, he was commissioned to collect all of Guevara writings into a compilation which was published by Editorial Diogenes. In 1971 and under the same publisher, he wrote his famous reportage named "[[Avándaro (book)|Avándaro]]" (with pictures by renown photographer [[Graciela Iturbide]]) which had heavy criticism of the [[Avándaro festival|rock festival of the same name]] from a leftist point of view, since he perceived that the [[jipitecas]] and [[La Onda]] members were not committed enough to change the country's political system as the leftists were and that they were being deceived by the government.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Carrion|first1=Luis|title=Avandaro|date=1971|publisher=Diogenes|edition=1st}}</ref>

title: Miriam Kochan
comment: clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: ’s → 's, A economics → An economics
diff_added:
'''Miriam Louise Kochan''' ({{nee|'''Buchler'''}}; 5 October 1929 – 1 January 2018) was an English history writer and translator of French. An economics graduate of the [[University of Exeter|University College of the South West]], she was the first female graduate of the [[Reuters]] news agency, working as sub-editor of the Reuters Economic Services from 1950 to 1954. Kochan was the translator of some 20 books that were originally published in French and she was the author of six books on popular history. In 1970, she introduced the [[Bar and bat mitzvah|bat mitzvah]] class for girls who were aged 12 and over in the British orthodox Jewish community and taught at [[Carmel College, Oxfordshire]].
Following graduation,<ref name=OxfordMailObituary/> Kochan became the first female graduate of the [[Reuters]] news agency.<ref name=JewishChronicleObituary/><ref name=GuardianObituary/> She was the sub-editor of the Reuters Economic Services from 1950 to 1954,<ref name=ContemporaryAuthorsEntry/> and was promoted from editorial assistant to journalist.<ref name=OxfordMailObituary/> Kochan became an freelance writer in 1954,<ref name=ContemporaryAuthorsEntry/> moving to [[Edinburgh]] in 1959, then [[Norwich]] in 1964 and finally [[Oxford]] in 1969.<ref name=GuardianObituary/>
She was the secretary of the Edinburgh Jewish Literary Society and taught at [[Carmel College, Oxfordshire]].<ref name=JewishChronicleObituary/> Kochan was heavily involved with the Oxford Jewish Community and eventually became its president.<ref name=JewishChronicleObituary/><ref name=OxfordMailObituary/> and introduced the [[GCE Ordinary Level|O-level syllabus]] (later [[GCSE]]) to the [[cheder]] (Hebrew class) in Oxford and she began the [[Bar and bat mitzvah|bat mitzvah]] class for girls who were aged 12 and over in the British orthodox Jewish community in 1970 after overcoming resistance from conservative elements.<ref name=GuardianObituary/><ref name=OxfordMailObituary/> She also introduced children's services, the synagogue magazine ''Menorah'' and a post bar/bat mitzvah class,<ref name=JewishChronicleObituary/> and had organisational roles with the [[B'nai B'rith]] and the [[Women's International Zionist Organization]].<ref name=GuardianObituary/> Kochan began a local Judaism study group, spoke about Judaism with non-Jewish groups, kiddush bookings and organised the Friendship Club.<ref name=JewishChronicleObituary/>

title: German Resistance Memorial Center
diff_added: Nevertheless, the umbrella term "German Resistance" ({{lang|de|Deutscher Widerstand}}) is now widely used to describe all elements of opposition and resistance under the [[Orwellian]] [[Nazi Regime]], including the underground networks of the [[Social Democratic Party (Germany)|SPD]] and [[Communist Party of Germany|KPD]], [[dissident]] writers and intellectuals living a secret life of [[inner emigration]] and who defied [[censorship in Nazi Germany|government censorship]] by illegally circulated anti-Nazi [[samizdat]] literature like {{lang|de|[[Weiße Rose]]}} ({{gloss|White Rose}}), opposition activities of the [[Catholic resistance to Nazi Germany|Catholic Church]] and other Christian denominations such as the [[Confessing Church]], along with the resistance groups based in the civil service, intelligence organs and armed forces.
diff_removed: Nevertheless, the umbrella term "[[German resistance to Nazism|German Resistance]]" ({{lang|de|Deutscher Widerstand}}) is now widely used to describe all elements of opposition and resistance under the [[Orwellian]] [[Nazi Regime]], including the underground networks of the [[Social Democratic Party (Germany)|SPD]] and [[Communist Party of Germany|KPD]], [[dissident]] writers and intellectuals living a secret life of [[inner emigration]] and who defied [[censorship in Nazi Germany|government censorship]] by illegally circulated anti-Nazi [[samizdat]] literature like {{lang|de|[[Weiße Rose]]}} ({{gloss|White Rose}}), opposition activities of the [[Catholic resistance to Nazi Germany|Catholic Church]] and other Christian denominations such as the [[Confessing Church]], along with the resistance groups based in the civil service, intelligence organs and armed forces.

'''Geel''' ({{IPA|nl|ɣeːl|-|Nl-Geel.ogg}}) is a [[Municipalities of Belgium|municipality]] and [[City status in Belgium|city]]<ref>{{Cite web |title=Wet tot toekenning van de titel van stad aan de gemeenten Beringen, Bilzen, Blankenberge, Borgloon, Ciney, Couvin, Damme, Geel, Genepiën, Gistel, Halen, Hamont-Achel, Hannuit, Harelbeke, Herk-de-Stad, Lo-Reninge, Mesen, Oudenburg, Peer, Scherpenheuvel-Zichem, Vilvoorde en Walcourt |url=https://www.ejustice.just.fgov.be/eli/wet/1985/07/19/1985001298/staatsblad |date=19 July 1985 |language=nl |website=www.ejustice.just.fgov.be |publisher=[[Belgian Official Gazette]] |access-date=22 April 2025}}</ref> located in the [[Belgium|Belgian]] [[Provinces of Belgium|province]] of [[Antwerp (province)|Antwerp]], which acquired city status in the 1980s. It comprises Central-Geel which is composed of four old parishes or towns: Sint-Amand, Sint-Dimpna, Holven and Elsum. Further on around the center are the parish-towns of Ten Aard (N), Bel (E), Winkelomheide (SE), Stelen, Oosterlo and Zammel (S), Punt (SW) and Larum (W). In 2021, Geel had a total population of 40,781.<ref name="stat">{{cite web|url=https://statbel.fgov.be/sites/default/files/files/opendata/bevolking/sectoren/OPENDATA_SECTOREN_2021.xlsx |title=Bevolking per statistische sector - Sector 13008 |website=Statistics Belgium |access-date=10 May 2022 }}</ref> The total area is {{convert|109.85|km2|0|abbr=on}}. Geel's [[patron saint]], the Irish [[Saint Dymphna]], inspired the town's pioneering de-institutionalized method of care for the mentally ill.
In 1795, the Duchy of Brabant was dissolved and Geel made part of the French [[130 departments of the First French Empire|department]] of [[Deux-Nèthes]], the precursor of the present province of Antwerp. The nobles and clerics of the [[Ancien Régime]] gave way to a mayor, who was now answerable to the town's people. Geel actively participated in the [[Belgian Revolution]] of 1830, resulting in the award of an honorary flag from [[Leopold II of Belgium|King Leopold II]]. The 19th century also saw the building of several new churches at the demand of the various parishes. A major occurrence in the history of Geel is the [[battle of Geel]] (also referred to as the "Gheel bridgehead") in September 1944, one of the heaviest and bloodiest fights during the liberation of Belgium. Eventually the territory of Geel, and the smaller villages around it, were finally liberated on September 23, but it had claimed numerous military and civilian casualties.
Geel is well known for the early adoption of de-institutionalization in psychiatric care.<ref>Roosens, Eugeen. ''Mental patients in town life: Geel, Europe's first therapeutic community''. Beverly Hills, Sage Publications, 1979. {{ISBN|0-8039-1330-3}} - {{ISBN|0-8039-1331-1}}</ref><ref>"A Lunatic Village," by Maurice E. Fox. title: Begum Hazrat Mahal
diff_added:
=Husainiya Mir Mukhlis Hussain Rizvi=
[[Husainiya Mir Mukhlis Hussain Rizvi]] or Mir Mukhlis Hussain Rizvi, born in [[Zaidpur]] in 1795. He was noted as a loyal collectrate to Awadh's last Nawab [[Wajid Ali Shah]]. He built an Imambara in [[Zaidpur]] named after him Imambargah Husainiya Mir Mukhlis Hussain Rizvi in Zaidpur. He was shoot by the British governal-general Lord Dalhousie in 1857 (after the revolt) in Victoria Street, Lucknow during offering prayer (Namaz). He hold two titles Raees e Zaidpur and Raees e Aazam. Mir Mukhlis Hussain has one son named Ali Yousef. His grand son named Ali Shaheer also built an Imambargah named Imambargah Husainiya Mir Ali Shaheer Rizvi. A Imambargah in Mirchiya named Husainiya Mir Ali Zaheer Rizvi was also built by his grand son. He was a [[Shia Muslim]] (Rizvi). His grave remains unknown for historians. In 1855 Wajid Ali Shah gave him a Alam's cloth with gold, silver and gemstones art as a gift for serving loyaliy for Wajid Ali Shah.

title: Faltings' theorem
diff_added:
Let <math>C</math> be a [[Singular point of a curve|non-singular]] algebraic curve of [[genus (mathematics)|genus]] <math>g</math> over <math>\mathbb{Q}</math>. Then the set of rational points on <math>C</math> may be determined as follows:
* When <math>g=0</math>, there are either no points or infinitely many. In such cases, <math>C</math> may be handled as a [[conic section]].
* When <math>g=1</math>, if there are any points, then <math>C</math> is an [[elliptic curve]] and its rational points form a [[finitely generated abelian group]]. (This is ''Mordell's Theorem'', later generalized to the [[Mordell–Weil theorem]].) Moreover, [[Mazur's torsion theorem]] restricts the structure of the torsion subgroup.
* When <math>g>1</math>, according to Faltings' theorem, <math>C</math> has only a finite number of rational points.
diff_removed:
Let {{mvar|C}} be a [[Singular point of a curve|non-singular]] algebraic curve of [[genus (mathematics)|genus]] {{mvar|g}} over ℚ. Then the set of rational points on {{mvar|C}} may be determined as follows:
* When {{math|1=''g'' = 0}}, there are either no points or infinitely many. In such cases, {{mvar|C}} may be handled as a [[conic section]].
* When {{math|1=''g'' = 1}}, if there are any points, then {{mvar|C}} is an [[elliptic curve]] and its rational points form a [[finitely generated abelian group]]. (This is ''Mordell's Theorem'', later generalized to the [[Mordell–Weil theorem]].) Moreover, [[Mazur's torsion theorem]] restricts the structure of the torsion subgroup.
* When {{math|1=''g'' > 0}}, according to Faltings' theorem, {{mvar|C}} has only a finite number of rational points.

title: Theological notes
diff_added:
The '''theological notes''' designate a classification of certainty of beliefs in [[Catholic theology]]. While theological notes qualify positively beliefs and doctrines, said beliefs and doctrines are qualified negatively by [[theological censures]]. The theological notes' "enumeration, division and evaluation" vary between authors.<ref name="Rahner232">{{Cite book |last=Finisterhölzl |first=Johann |author-link= |title=Encyclopedia of Theology: The Concise Sacramentum Mundi |date=1975 |publisher=Seabury Press |isbn=978-0-86012-006-3 |editor-last=Rahner |editor-first=Karl |editor-link=Karl Rahner |pages=1681 |language=en |chapter=Theological notes |chapter-url=https://archive.org/details/encyclopediaofth0000unse_l8a2/page/1681/mode/2up}}</ref>
Examples of ''sententia communis'' beliefs which are cited by Ott include the power of [[saints]] in [[Heaven in Christianity|heaven]] to help the souls in [[purgatory]] by [[Intercession of saints|intercession]]<ref>{{cite book |last1=Ott |first1=Ludwig |title=Fundamentals of Catholic Dogma |page=322}}</ref> and the conviction that dead people cannot receive [[Sacraments of the Catholic Church|sacraments]].<ref>{{cite book |last1=Ott |first1=Ludwig |title=Fundamentals of Catholic Dogma |page=344}}</ref>

• Dead people cannot receive [[Sacraments of the Catholic Church|sacraments]]<ref>{{cite book |last1=Ott |first1=Ludwig |title=Fundamentals of Catholic Dogma |page=344}}</ref>

title: Information Awareness Office
Fields Medal
comment: 1. Avoid informal wording  Original:  has long been regarded as the most prestigious award  Edited:  is widely regarded as one of the most prestigious awards  Reason: Wikipedia avoids absolute claims like “the most prestigious” unless strongly sourced.  2. Removed reference numbers like [3][4][5]  Wikipedia references are added using <ref>...</ref> tags, not copied numbers in plain text.  3. Improved formal tone  Original:  it has an age limit: a recipient must be under age 40  Edited:  includes
diff_added:
Conditions of the award
The Fields Medal is widely regarded as one of the most prestigious awards in the field of mathematics and is often described as the “Nobel Prize of Mathematics”. However, unlike the Nobel Prize, the Fields Medal is awarded once every four years and includes an age restriction: recipients must be under 40 years of age on 1 January of the year in which the medal is awarded.

The age limit reflects the intention of John Charles Fields that the award should not only recognize outstanding work already completed, but also encourage further achievement by the recipients and stimulate renewed effort among other mathematicians. In addition, an individual may receive the Fields Medal only once and is not eligible for future awards..<ref>{{cite web |url=http://darboux.mathunion.org/fileadmin/IMU/Prizes/Fields/Statutes%20Fields%20Medal.pdf |title=Rules for the Fields Medal |website=mathunion.org |access-date=1 May 2018 |archive-date=2 May 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180502070440/http://darboux.mathunion.org/fileadmin/IMU/Prizes/Fields/Statutes%20Fields%20Medal.pdf |url-status=live }}</ref>
diff_removed:
==Conditions of the award==
The Fields Medal has long been regarded as the most prestigious award in the field of mathematics and is often [[List of prizes known as the Nobel of a field|described as the Nobel Prize of Mathematics]].<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":3" /> However, unlike the Nobel Prize,<ref>{{Cite journal |last=Klainerman |first=Sergiu |date=2015 |title=Is the Fields Medal the Nobel Prize of Mathematics? |url=https://www.ams.org/notices/201504/rnoti-p327.pdf |journal=Notices of the American Mathematical Society |volume=62 |issue=4 |pages=327 |issn=0002-9920 |access-date=28 June 2022 |archive-date=28 June 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220628180534/https://www.ams.org/notices/201504/rnoti-p327.pdf |url-status=live }}</ref> the Fields Medal is only awarded every four years, and it has an age limit: a recipient must be under age 40 on 1 January of the year in which the medal is awarded. The under-40 rule is based on Fields's desire that "while it was in recognition of work already done, it was at the same time intended to be an encouragement for further achievement on the part of the recipients and a stimulus to renewed effort on the part of others."<ref>{{Harvnb|McKinnon Riehm|Hoffman|2011|p=183}}</ref> Moreover, an individual can only be awarded one Fields Medal; winners are ineligible to be awarded future medals.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://darboux.mathunion.org/fileadmin/IMU/Prizes/Fields/Statutes%20Fields%20Medal.pdf |title=Rules for the Fields Medal |website=mathunion.org |access-date=1 May 2018 |archive-date=2 May 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180502070440/http://darboux.mathunion.org/fileadmin/IMU/Prizes/Fields/Statutes%20Fields%20Medal.pdf |url-status=live }}</ref>

title: Thomas Vander Wal
diff_added:
Much of his work, including the Personal InfoCloud and folksonomies, is undergirded by what he calls the Model of Attraction. MoA is a metaphor, much like navigation or sense of smell, that helps information architects and web developers think about the way that users interact with information. To think about MoA in relation to folksonomies, certain terms are associated with different pieces of information. The strength of the association can be visualized as a magnetic field, bringing certain kinds of information around a term. When a user searches on that term, they enter into the magnetic field. To think about MoA in relation to the Personal InfoCloud, a user's associated information and interests create a similar magnetic field, which draws pertinent information to them, and keeps it around them.<ref>{{cite web |last=Vander Wal |first=Thomas |title=Model of Attraction |url=http://vanderwal.net/essays/moa1.html |accessdate=29 February 2012}}</ref>
Vander Wal works at InfoCloud Solutions as the Principal and Senior Consultant.<ref>{{cite web |title=About InfoClouds Solutions |url=http://infocloudsolutions.com/about.php}}</ref> InfoClouds Solutions is Vander Wal's consulting company that advises on the range of digital content/media, folksonomy/tagging, social web, and personal to social information use and reuse.

title: 279th Army Field Support Brigade
diff_added:
The '''279th Army Field Support Brigade''' is a unit of the [[Alabama Army National Guard]] since 2008 when the 151st Combat Engineer Battalion and the 279th Antiaircraft Automatic Weapons Battalion were merged to create the 279th Signal Battalion in 1959.<ref>{{Cite web |title=U.S. ARMY 279TH ARMY FIELD SUPPORT BRIGADE PATCH (SSI) |url=http://www.uniforms-4u.com/p-us-army-279th-support-brigade-patch-13617.aspx |access-date=July 12, 2025 |website=The Salute Uniforms}}</ref>  The units predecessor units served in Alaska in World War II and in Korea.  The 279th Signal served during the [[Berlin Crisis of 1961|Berlin Crisis]] in 1961.<ref>{{Cite web |date=January 15, 2013 |title=The 279th Army Field Support Brigade, Alabama NG |url=https://media-cdn.dvidshub.net/pubs/pdf_48596.pdf |website=Army Sustainment Command}}</ref> On September 1, 2008, the unit was redesignated as a Brigade.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |date=January 15, 2013 |title=ASC History Newsletter |url=https://d34w7g4gy10iej.cloudfront.net/pubs/pdf_48596.pdf |website=DVIDS}}</ref>
Subordinate units of the brigade deployed to Bagram airfield in Afghanistan in 2009 and 2010 and to Kandahar airfield in 2012.<ref name=":0" />
The mission of the unit is to support US [[NORTHCOM]] providing logistics support to whatever mission they are given.

title: Pictionary (1989 game show)
diff_added:
'''''Pictionary''''' is an American children's [[game show]] based on the picture-drawing [[Pictionary|board game]] of the same name. This was the first of three game shows based on the board game, with later editions for adults launched in [[Pictionary (1997 game show)|1997]] and [[Pictionary (2022 game show)|2022]]. The show was hosted by [[Brian Robbins]], with assistance from Julie Friedman as "Felicity", and [[Rick Zumwalt]] as "Judge Mental".<ref>{{cite book | chapter-url=https://books.google.com/books?id=siG2EAAAQBAJ&dq=%22brian+robbins%22+%22pictionary%22&pg=PA76 | title=Game Show Confidential: The Story of an American Obsession | author=Boze Hadleigh | year=2023 | chapter=Games People Play| publisher=Rowman & Littlefield | isbn=978-1-4930-7259-0 }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=BG64AAAAIAAJ&q=%22brian+robbins%22+%22pictionary%22 | title=Pictionary | journal=Television/Radio Age | year=1988 | pages=61}}</ref><ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.newspapers.com/image/737657959/ | title=The hipper host | work=The News Tribune | date=June 17, 1989 | accessdate=October 23, 2024 | author=Ian Harmer | pages=13}}</ref>
''Pictionary'' ran from June 5, 1989, until September 8, 1989, in syndication and was a joint production of [[Barry & Enright Productions]] and QMI Television.  The program was distributed by [[NBCUniversal Syndication Studios|MCA Television]].
Harry Stevens, who had just finished announcing for the syndicated version of the Nickelodeon game show ''[[Finders Keepers (American game show)|Finders Keepers]]'', filled the same role on ''Pictionary'' while arm wrestler [[Rick Zumwalt]] served as the game's rules enforcer and judge as the character of “Judge Mental”.

title: Murder of Joshua Moyers
comment: clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: an unanimous → a unanimous (2)
diff_added:
Nassau County Sheriff's Deputy Joshua Joseph Moyers was born on August 6, 1992, and he originated from [[Kings Ferry, Florida]]. Deputy Moyers completed his high school education in Hilliard Middle-Senior High School, before he further his studies in university, earning a bachelor's degree in Public Safety Management at Florida State College and also graduating from Saint Johns River State College in 2013. Deputy Moyers first joined the police force in 2011, first becoming a Sheriff's Office Explorer from early 2011 to May 2013, and later promoted to a deputy in 2015. On July 14, 2018, Deputy Moyers was awarded the Above and the Beyond the Call of Duty award for his investigation of a narcotics case.<ref>{{cite news |title=Family of Deputy Joshua Moyers thanks law enforcement, community for 'outpouring of love'. |date=September 30, 2021 |work=News 4 Jax |url=https://www.news4jax.com/news/local/2021/09/30/family-of-deputy-joshua-moyers-thanks-law-enforcement-community-for-outpouring-of-love/ }}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Nassau County deputy Joshua Moyers died after being shot at a traffic stop. Here's what we know. |date=September 27, 2021 |work=The Florida Times-Union |url=https://www.jacksonville.com/story/news/crime/2021/09/27/florida-deputy-joshua-moyers-dead-after-traffic-stop-shooting-patrick-mcdowell-what-we-know/5883096001/ }}</ref>
When Deputy Moyers approached the vehicle, McDowell gave the officer a false name due to the fact that he had outstanding warrants for his arrest for unrelated charges. Deputy Moyers asked McDowell to get out of the vehicle, and armed with his handgun, McDowell fired two shots at the police officer, first shooting Deputy Moyers in the face below his eye, before firing a second shot at Deputy Moyers's back as he fell to the ground. After the shooting, McDowell fled the scene.<ref>{{cite news |title=Judge sentences Nassau County deputy's killer to death. |date=July 17, 2024 |work=News 4 Jax |url=https://www.news4jax.com/news/local/2024/07/17/nassau-county-deputys-killer-to-be-sentenced-wednesday-after-jury-recommended-death-penalty/ }}</ref>
Aside from this, the defence also filed a motion to allow the jury to only impose the death penalty by a unanimous decision. At that time, the state of Florida had changed its laws to allow the death penalty be imposed by a majority vote from at least eight jurors or more, therefore invalidating the state's previous requirement for solely a unanimous recommendation for capital punishment. The new laws took effect in April 2024, a month after McDowell pleaded guilty to the murder of Deputy Moyers, and the defence asked for the law to not apply retroactively in McDowell's case.<ref>{{cite news |title=Jury unanimity at center of death penalty case of Nassau County murderer. |date=February 23, 2024 |work=First Coast News |url=https://www.firstcoastnews.com/article/news/local/murderer-of-nassau-county-deputy-wants-unanimous-jury-to-decide-his-fate/77-ec5b755d-19e6-41c7-a980-ba5

title: Moonbound
comment: /* Plot */ clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: aren’t → aren't, ’s → 's
diff_added: When Peter sets out on a magical journey to rescue his little sister Anne, he needs to travel to mysterious territory: the Moon! Anne was kidnapped by the evil Moon Man when she tried to help the beetle Mr. Zoomzeman in search for his wife. On his fantastic adventure, Peter lands on the Star Meadow where he meets the sleepy Mr. Sandman. He knows, only at the Night Fairy's dinner in the castle in the clouds, they can find out where Anne is – but there aren't enough seats for everyone. So they join the wild race along the Milky Way against the five Spirits of Nature: Storm Giant, Lightning Witch, Henry Hail, Rainy Robin and Mother Frost...

title: Jack Komakech

'''Jack Komakech''' (born July 31, 2002) is a Uganda professional football player who plays as a goalkeeper currently playing for [[Vipers SC]] and also the [[Uganda national football team|Uganda National Men's Football team]] (Uganda Cranes).<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-02-26 |title=Uganda Hippos living Afcon dream |url=https://www.monitor.co.ug/uganda/sports/soccer/uganda-hippos-living-afcon-dream--3305918 |access-date=2025-07-19 |website=Monitor |language=en}}</ref> He won Fortebet Real Stars Monthly Accolade for February 2021.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |last=Isabirye |first=David |date=2021-03-03 |title=Jack Komakech's mother initially stopped him from playing football |url=https://kawowo.com/2021/03/03/jack-komakechs-mother-initially-stopped-him-from-playing-football/ |access-date=2025-07-19 |website=Kawowo Sports |language=en-US}}</ref>
Komakech was born to Billy Okot and Achira Florence Okot in [[Gulu District]], [[Northern Region, Uganda|Northern Uganda]].<ref name=":0" /> He went to Ascent Soccer Academy where he became the first-choice goalkeeper for the Uganda U-17 and U-20 National Teams.<ref name=":0" /> He also attended Kings Way High School in Entebbe for his secondary school education before join St. Mary's Senior Secondary School.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=Abdusalam |first=Kigozi |date=2021-08-30 |title=Jack Komakech Pens Five-year Deal With UPL Giants |url=https://chimpreports.com/jack-komakech-pens-five-year-deal-with-upl-giants/ |access-date=2025-07-19 |website=ChimpReports |language=en-US}}</ref>
Komakech started his football career with Ascent Soccer Academy before join U-17 and U-20 National Teams.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Isabirye |first=David |date=2020-09-16 |title=Jack Komakech: Uganda U-20 goalkeeper targeted by South African top club |url=https://kawowo.com/2020/09/16/jack-komakech-uganda-u-20-goalkeeper-targeted-by-south-african-top-club/ |access-date=2025-07-19 |website=Kawowo Sports |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Mbabazi |first=Grace Lindsay |title=Bashir Mutanda's COSAFA dream shattered by knee injury; Birungi replaces him |url=https://nilepost.co.ug/news/44750/bashir-mutandas-cosafa-dream-shattered-by-knee-injurybirungi-replaces-him |access-date=2025-07-19 |website=Nilepost News |language=en}}</ref> Komakech signed a contract with [[Vipers SC]] aa head of 2021/2022 season replacing Sekajja Bashir.<ref name=":1" />

'''The System''' is a [[Glossary of professional wrestling terms#Stable|professional wrestling stable]] that performs in [[Total Nonstop Action Wrestling]] (TNA). The group consists of second leader [[Eddie Edwards (wrestler)|Eddie Edwards]], [[Brian Myers]], [[Bear Bronson]], and [[Cedric Alexander]]. As a stable, [[Moose (wrestler)|Moose]], who was the original leader, is a former one-time [[TNA X Division Championship|TNA X Division Champion]] and a former [[TNA World Champion]], Myers and Eddie are former two-time [[TNA World Tag Team Champions]], and former members [[Alisha Edwards]] and [[Masha Slamovich]] are former [[TNA Knockouts World Tag Team Champion]]s.
On January 13, 2024, at the "Countdown to [[Hard To Kill (2024)|TNA Hard To Kill]]", Myers and Edwards defeated [[Eric Young (wrestler)|Eric Young]] and [[Frankie Kazarian]] pre-show. During a backstage interview at Hard To Kill, Edwards, Myers, [[Alisha Edwards]], Moose and [[DeAngelo Williams]] would form a stable calling themselves "The System". The stable later accompanied Moose to his main event match for the TNA World Championship against Alex Shelley. During the match, Edwards and the rest of The System attempted to distract Shelley, prompting a brawl with Shelley's partners, Chris Sabin and Kushida. Moose later won the match, after which he was confronted by [[Dolph Ziggler|Nic Nemeth]].<ref name="HTK2024">{{cite web|last=Powell|first=Jason|title=TNA Hard To Kill results: Powell's live review of Alex Shelley vs. Moose for the TNA Title, Trinity vs. Jordynne Grace for the Knockouts Title, Josh Alexander vs. Alex Hammerstone|url=https://prowrestling.net/site/2024/01/13/tna-hard-to-kill-results-powells-live-review-of-alex-shelley-vs-moose-for-the-tna-title-trinity-vs-jordynne-grace-for-the-knockouts-title-josh-alexander-vs-alex-hammerstone/|work=Pro Wrestling Dot Net|date=January 13, 2024|access-date=January 13, 2024}}</ref> On February 23, at the "Countdown to [[No Surrender (2024)|No Surrender]]", Myers and Edwards defeated Intergalactic Jet Setters ([[Kevin Knight (wrestler)|Kevin Knight]] and Kushida).<ref name="NoSurrender2024">{{cite web|title=No Surrender 2024 on TNA+ Results|url=https://tnawrestling.com/2024/02/23/no-surrender-2024-on-tna-live-results/|publisher=TNA Wrestling|author=TNA Wrestling Staff|date=February 23, 2024|access-date=February 23, 2024}}</ref><ref name="NS2024">{{cite web|last=Vetter|first=Chris|title=TNA No Surrender results (2/23): Vetter’s review of Moose vs. Alex Shelley for in a No Surrender Rules match for the TNA Title, ABC vs. GYV in the final match of the best of three series for the TNA Tag Titles|url=https://prowrestling.net/site/2024/02/24/tna-no-surrender-results-2-23-vetters-review-of-moose-vs-alex-shelley-for-in-a-no-surrender-rules-match-for-the-tna-title-abc-vs-gyv-in-the-final-match-of-the-best-of-three-series-for-the-tna/|work=Pro Wrestling Dot Net|date=February 24, 2024|access-date=February 24, 2

title: State road D.060 (Turkey)
diff_added:
'''D-060''' is a west–east [[trunk road|state road]] in northeastern [[Turkey]] that serves the land border crossing to [[Armenia]], which is currently closed. The {{convert|225|km|mi|abbr=on}} route starts in the west at the intersection of [[State road D.950 (Turkey)|D.950]], {{convert|18|km|mi|abbr=on}} east of [[Yusufeli]], [[Artvin Province]], and terminates at Turkey's Tiknis [[border checkpoint]] with [[Armenia]] {{convert|12|km|mi|abbr=on}} east of [[Akyaka, Kars]].<ref name="kgm"/> The route D-060 continues in Armenia as [[M7 Highway (Armenia)|M7]].
*{{jct|country=TUR|D|965 }}

title: El Coyote (character)
diff_added:
Several of the novels' characters appeared throughout the series. Among them, Fray Jacinto at the mission station [[San Juan Capistrano, California|San Juan de Capistrano]], who knew of Coyote's identity (and tried to get César to marry Lupe – he was the only one knowing Julian's true background. He also encouriged Coyote to let Jr know of his father's double identity. Fray Jacinto was murdered in late 1872 (in ''Los servidores del circulo verde'', number 45). Later there was Fray Anselmo at [[San Benito County|San Benito de Palermo]]. Don Goyo Paz fought in the Mexican–American War and was a ranch owner not too far from San Antonio – los Lugones were hired bodyguards at Don Goyo's, whose son Gregorio Paz was to marry Guadalupe, but "El Diablo" forced César to marry her instead.<ref>Guadalupe already had fallen in love with her employer before she found out his secret identity - and Coyote himself later became very fond of her and really didn't protest to the marriage</ref> There were solicitor José Covarrubias, doctor Garcia Oviedo, and Los Angeles sheriff Teodomiro Mateos – sheriff twice (from late 1865 and shortly also in the early 1870s), and in later years one of César's best friends. Of those three at least Oviedo knew the identity of Coyote from 1871.
Former civil war spy Ginevra St. Clair appeared around the late 1860s (''El exterminio de la Calavera'', number 9, where César fell in love with her, but she was killed). It is considered one of the most appreciated novels in the series. There also were three more adventurers who appeared in several novels – Chris Wardell, "El Diablo" (Juan Nepomuceno Mariñas - later known as Roberto Cifuentes), and "Princess Irina" (Odile Garson – she debuted in ''Otra lucha'', number 13, in early 1870, and soon tried to charm César, but ended up as the partner of "El Diablo" after César's marriage). All three started out as some kind of enemies of Coyote.  Anita was Guadalupe's maiden, and [[San Francisco]] chief [[vigilante]] Captain Farrell became a good friend of Coyote.<ref>"Frisco" was much larger than L.A. in those days and had almost 25 times the population in 1870, with 30.000 in 1850</ref> Another noble ranch owner was Don Rómulo Hidalgo (with his son Justo – his father was murdered around 1871). In the novels there were of course also a lot of villains –  Coyote's worst and toughest enemy probably was Robert Toombs (featured in ''Guadalupe'', original Cliper novel number 47 and its sequels, telling stories around 1872).<ref>All the main and important characters appeared in the original 130 pulps (and re-appeared in several of the "Nuevo Coyote" novels). The datings are either picked directly from the novels or approximately interpreted through the stories' chronology (dates are a few times slightly conflicting in the various novels)</ref> Several novels were adventures with sequels (one special long series was Coyote's adventures with Analupe de Monreal in 1872-1873).

{{Use dmy dates|date=March 2026}}
'''Kwon Pyong''' ({{Korean|hangul=권평}}; born 1988), also known as '''Quan Ping''' ({{Lang-zh|s=权平}}) or '''Johnny Kwon''', is a Chinese rights activist. He was charged in 2017 with inciting subversion, and served an 18-month prison sentence. In 2023, he fled to South Korea via [[jet ski]], and was detained on immigration charges. He was given a suspended prison sentence, and was deported to the United States in 2024, where he planned to [[Asylum seeker|plead asylum]].
Kwon was born in 1988, to an ethnically Korean family in [[Yanbian Korean Autonomous Prefecture]], China.<ref name=":0">{{Cite news |last=Hawkins |first=Amy |last2= |first2= |date=22 November 2023 |title=Xi critic who fled 300km on jetski to South Korea will die if sent back to China, says father |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2023/nov/22/xi-critic-who-fled-on-jetski-to-south-korea-will-die-if-sent-back-to-china-says-father |access-date=7 March 2026 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref> He attended [[Iowa State University]] in the United States, studying aerospace engineering, and graduated in either 2012<ref name=":0" /> or 2014.<ref name=":3">{{Cite news |last=Yoon |first=John |date=24 August 2023 |title=Escape China by Jet Ski? A Dissident Is Said to Have Planned It for Years. |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/08/24/world/asia/china-jet-ski-dissident-escape.html |access-date=7 March 2026 |work=The New York Times |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite news |last=Buckley |first=Chris |date=16 February 2017 |title=He Called China's President 'Xitler' on Twitter. Now He Faces Prison. |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2017/02/16/world/asia/china-xi-jinping-xitler-tshirt-kwon-pyong.html |access-date=7 March 2026 |work=The New York Times |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331}}</ref> At some point, he also attended the [[University of Queensland]] in Australia.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ng |first=Yik-tung |last2=Lee |first2=Lai |last3=Mudie |first3=Luisetta |date=8 December 2016 |title=Xi Jinping T-Shirt Activist Confirmed Held in Northeast China Detention Center |url=https://www.rfa.org/english/news/china/xi-jinping-tshirt-activist-confirmed-held-in-northeast-china-detention-center-12082016110338.html |access-date=7 March 2026 |website=Radio Free Asia |language=en}}</ref> During his student years, he maintained a blog and praised the democratic conduct of the [[2012 United States presidential election|2012 U.S. presidential election]]. According to his father, he grew critical of China's one-party system during his time abroad.<ref name=":0" />
In 2016, Kwon posted a picture of himself outside a government building, wearing a shirt mocking Chinese leader [[Xi Jinping's cult of personality|Xi Jinping]]. The shirt referred to Xi as "Xitler", "[[Rubao culture|Xi Baozi]]", and "Big Spender".<ref name=":10">{{Cite news |last=Lai |first=Catherine |date=3 November 2016 |title=Young Chinese activist missing after sharing plan

title: New Zealand DC class locomotive
diff_added: Each locomotive has a General Motors [[EMD 645|12-645C]] or 12-645E V12 diesel engine (the same as originally fitted to the DF class) and four traction motors, with an authorised maximum speed of 100 km/h. title: Ephraim Porter Felt
diff_added: Felt worked mostly with [[Nematocera]], particularly [[Cecidomyiidae]]. However, as State Entomologist for New York, the scope of his work included all insects of [[economic entomology|economic]] or [[Medical entomology|medical]] significance. He wrote ''Insects Affecting Park and Woodland Trees'' (New York State Museum Memoir 8, 1905–1906), ''Control of Household Insects'' (New York State Museum Bulletin No. 129, May 1906), and ''Plant Galls and Gall Makers'' (Ithaca, N.Y., Comstock Pub. Co., 1940), and described over 1,000 [[species]] in scientific journals. He described the mosquito genus ''[[Culiseta]]''. The [[holotype]]s of the Felt-named insect species are in the [[National Museum of Natural History]].<ref>{{cite journal|author=Howard, L.O.|authorlink=Leland Ossian Howard|year=1930 |title=History of applied Entomology (Somewhat Anecdotal)|journal= Smiths. Miscell. Coll.|volume= 84. |url=https://repository.si.edu/handle/10088/23848|pages=1–564}}</ref>

title: Google Maps
diff_added: '''Google Maps''' is a [[web mapping]] platform and consumer application developed by [[Google]]. It offers [[satellite imagery]], [[aerial photography]], street maps, 360° [[interactive panorama|interactive panoramic]] views of streets ([[Google Street View|Street View]]), real-time traffic conditions, and [[route planner|route planning]] for traveling by foot, car, bike, and [[public transportation]]. {{As of|2020}}, Google Maps was being used by over one billion people every month around the world.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Google Maps Metrics and Infographics |url= https://youtube.com/@h2oremix88?si=YELl4XsJEyzUliEc/a/pressatgoogle.com/google-maps-for-iphone/google-maps-metrics|access-date=2021-04-01|website=Google Maps for iPhone |archive-date=March 21, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220321192815/https://sites.google.com/a/pressatgoogle.com/google-maps-for-iphone/google-maps-metrics|url-status=live}}</ref>

title: Ganesh Kumar Mani
comment: clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: Managing Director → managing director, Chairman → chairman, 2001-2003 → 2001–2003 (7)
diff_added:
Mani started his career as a faculty member at the [[Christian Medical College and Hospital]], Vellore<ref name="Bharat Top 10" /> and moved as the Specialist Surgeon to the Indian Railways Southern Railway HQ Hospital in [[Perambur]] in the south Indian state of Tamil Nadu,<ref name="Bharat Top 10" /> in 1984. He worked there till 1989 when he returned to Delhi as the Chief Cardiac Surgeon and Director of Cardiac Surgery at the Batra Hospital and Medical Research Centre,<ref name="Bharat Top 10" /> a position he held till 1995.<ref name="DTEA Profile" /> The next move was as the Senior Cardiothoracic Consultant of the [[Apollo Hospital, Indraprastha]], a 700 bedded tertiary care hospital in the Indian capital,<ref name="Apollo">{{cite web | url=http://www.apollohospdelhi.com/apollo-delhi | title=Apollo | publisher=Apollo | date=2014 | access-date=25 October 2014}}</ref><ref name="Bharat Top 10" /><ref name="India Medical Times" /> working there from 1996 to 2003.<ref name="DTEA Profile" /> He has also worked as the visiting consultant at hospitals such as Saroj Hospital,<ref name="Bharat Top 10" /> Delhi (2001–2003), Gandhi Medical College, Bhopal (2003), Delhi Heart and Lung Institute (2003–2009), Golden Heart Institute of the Jaipur Golden Hospital (2005–2009), the Mool Chand Hospital, Delhi (2008–2009) and as the Director of the Pushpanjali Institute of Cardiac Sciences and Crosslay Remedies.<ref name="DTEA Profile" /><ref name="Bharat Top 10" /><ref name="Saket City Hospital" />
In 2013, Mani moved to Saket City Hospital, Delhi and is the chairman, Cardiothoracic Vascular Surgery, there.<ref name="Saket City Hospital">{{cite web | url=http://www.saketcityhospital.com/doctor/Dr.-Ganesh-K-Mani | title=Saket City Hospital | publisher=Saket City Hospital | date=2014 | access-date=25 October 2014 | archive-date=27 March 2015 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150327122229/http://www.saketcityhospital.com/doctor/Dr.-Ganesh-K-Mani | url-status=dead }}</ref> He is also the managing director of ''Mani Heart Care'', a facility he founded in 1990.<ref name="Mani Heart Care">{{cite web | url=http://corporatedir.com/company/mani-heart-care-private-limited | title=Mani Heart Care | publisher=Mani Heart Care | date=2014 | access-date=25 October 2014}}</ref><ref name="DTEA Profile" />
Ganesh Mani is a former member of the executive council of the Delhi Medical Council (1998–2004), a former member of the Medical Advisory Board Chronic Care Foundation (2003–2010)<ref name="Bharat Top 10" /> and is the President of the Health Education And Research Trust, Delhi and a National Committee member of the Indian Healthcare Federation.<ref name="DTEA Profile" /><ref name="Bharat Top 10" />
Mani has been honoured by many institutions and organizations. He was a member of the [[Indian Antarctic Program|9th Indian Scientific Expedition to Antarctica]], in 1989–90.<ref name="DTEA Profile" /><ref name="Bharat Top 10" /> The [[Government of India]], in 2013, confe

Ganesh Mani is a former member of the executive council of the Delhi Medical Council (1998-2004), a former member of the Medical Advisory Board Chronic Care Foundation (2003-2010)<ref name="Bharat Top 10" /> and is the President of the Health Education And Research Trust, Delhi and a National Committee member of the Indian Healthcare Federation.<ref name="DTEA Profile" /><ref name="Bharat Top 10" />

title: Carol Cartwright
diff_added:
'''Carol A. Cartwright''' is an American academic administrator who served as president of [[Kent State University]] and, later, of [[Bowling Green State University]].[[File:CartwrightHallKSU.jpg|thumb|Cartwright Hall at Kent State University was named after Carol Cartwright<ref>{{cite web |last1=Kano |first1=Krista |title=Kent State names buildings for FedEx, president Warren |url=https://www.record-courier.com/news/20190509/kent-state-names-buildings-for-fedex-president-warren |website=Record-Courier |access-date=9 October 2019 |language=en}}</ref>]]
Cartwright served as the 10th president of [[Kent State University]] from March 1991 until July 2006, making her the first woman to serve as president of any Ohio public college or university.<ref name="kent.edu">Abraham, Lisa [https://www.kent.edu/today/news/madame-president-carol-cartwright "Madame President: Carol A. Cartwright"], "kent.edu", March 3, 2023, accessed September 30, 2024</ref><ref>Galbincea, Barb [http://blog.cleveland.com/metro/2009/01/cartwright_kents_fformer_presi.html "Carol Cartwright, former Kent State president, gets top job at Bowling Green"], ''Cleveland.com'', January 06, 2009, accessed December 15, 2010.</ref>
During her tenure at Kent State, she oversaw the establishment of new colleges and academic programs, the introduction of new technologies, and increases in enrollment; she also oversaw an extensive the extensive construction of new campus buildings, including the Centennial Court dormitories and a new honors college complex, and the renovation of several others.<ref name="kent.edu"/> She also oversaw the completion of the university's first major fund-raising campaign.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |date=2024-09-24 |title=Dr. Carol A. Cartwright |url=https://www.knightcommission.org/bios/carol-a-cartwright/ |access-date=2024-11-07 |website=Knight Commission on Intercollegiate Athletics |language=en-US}}</ref>
Upon retirement from Kent State, the auditorium building on the university's Kent campus was renamed Cartwright Hall in her honor.<ref name=":0" />
Cartwright served on the [[Knight Commission]] beginning in 2000,<ref name=":0" /> and in 2010 she was elected as vice chair to [[NPR]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Rehm |first1=Dana Davis |title=Meet New NPR Board Chair and Vice Chair, and NPR Foundation Board Chair |url=https://www.npr.org/sections/npr-extra/2010/11/17/131388015/meet-new-npr-board-chair-and-vice-chair-and-npr-foundation-board-chair |website=NPR.org |date=17 November 2010 |access-date=8 July 2019 |language=en}}</ref> Cartwright has served on the boards of directors of KeyCorp (retiring in 2012), Republic Engineered Steels, Inc. (1992–98) the [[Davey Tree Expert Company]] (2002–08), [[PolyOne Corporation]], and [[FirstEnergy]] Corporation.<ref name=":0" /> She currently sits on the Board of Trustees of [[Heidelberg University]].
The scope of her contributions to higher education led to Dr. Cartwright's induction into the Ohio Women's Hall of Fame in her firs

It can be found at depths from {{convert|5|to|10|m|ft|abbr=on}}.  It can reach a maximum total length of {{convert|8|cm|in|abbr=on}}.<ref>{{FishBase |genus= Sticharium|species= clarkae| month = April | year = 2013}}</ref> The [[Specific name (zoology)|specific name]] of this  clinid honours  [[ichthyologist]]  [[Eugenie Clark]] (1922–2015) of the University of Maryland.<ref>{{cite web | url = http://www.etyfish.org/blenniiformes3/ | title = Order BLENNIIFORMES: Families CLINIDAE, LABRISOMIDAE and CHAENOPSIDAE | work = ETYFish Fish Name Etymology Database | author1 = Christopher Scharpf | author2 = Kenneth J. title: Thoroughbred breeding theories
diff_added:
'''[[Thoroughbred]] breeding theories''', or '''racehorse theories''', are used by [[horse breeder]]s in an attempt to arrange matings that produce progeny successful in [[horse racing]]. Bloodstock experts also rely on these theories when purchasing young horses or breeding stock. A basic understanding of these theories can also help the racing public understand a horse's theoretical genetic potential. The breeding theories stem from the belief that careful analysis of [[heredity|bloodlines]] can lend predictability to breeding outcomes. A well-designed mating increases the ''probability'' of the offspring's success, although many other factors also come into play.
{{main|Dosage Index}}
During the 1950s Kaziemierz Bobinski and Count Zamoyski produced ''Family Tables of Racehorses,''<ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.bloodlines.net/TB/Notes/ReferenceBooks.htm |title= Reference Books |access-date=2008-02-14 |work=Bloodlines}}</ref> commonly known as the Bobinski Tables. This work expanded Bruce Lowe's numbering system of 43 families and identified a total of 74 families tracing to mares in the GSB.<ref name="mtDNA1">{{cite web |url= http://www.tbheritage.com/GeneticMarkers/mtdnaintbdamlines.html |title= New Research Sheds Light on Old Pedigrees |access-date=2008-02-17 |author= Erigero, Patricia  |work= Genetic Markers |publisher=Thoroughbred Heritage}} which cites {{cite journal |author=Hill, E. W.|year=2002 |title= History and Integrity of Thoroughbred Dam Lines Revealed in Equine mtDNA Variation |journal=Animal Genetics |volume=33  |pages=287–294  |url= http://www.tbheritage.com/GeneticMarkers/HillAnimalGenetics.pdf |access-date=2008-02-17 |doi= 10.1046/j.1365-2052.2002.00870.x|issue=4 |pmid=12139508|display-authors=etal}}</ref> They identified mares in several countries whose pedigrees had been lost or whose descendants were unacceptable to the GSB at the time of Lowe's work. Bobinski later updated his works and split Lowe's families into sub categories.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.bloodlines.net/TB/Families/Family1.htm |title= Family Tables |access-date=2008-02-17 |work=Bloodlines}}</ref> The current female family tables were updated by Toru Shirai of the Japanese Bloodstock Agency with the latest update occurring in 2004.
Today, these numbers often follow a horse's name in sale catalogues and pedigrees, much like a numerical surname and are used for checking the accuracy of pedigrees and comparing the contributions made by various mares and families.<ref name="Wicks">{{cite book  | last = Wicks| first = B.M| title = The Australian Racehorse: An Introduction to Breeding | publisher = Libra Books  | year = 1973 | location = Canberra|page=16}}</ref>  Horses that come from more highly respected families will usually command better prices than those from less respected bloodlines, although they may not prove to be better as racehorses or sires/broodmares.<ref name="Napier">{{cite book  | last = Napier  | first = Miles  | title = Blood will tell:

title: Idempotence
diff_added:
===Idempotent morphism===
A morphism <math>f : x \to x</math> in a category <math>C</math> is called ''idempotent'' if <math>f \circ f = f</math>.<ref>Ch. I., § 5. in {{Cite book|title=Categories for the Working Mathematician|last=Mac Lane|first=Saunders|date=1978|publisher=Springer New York|isbn=1441931236|edition=Second|location=New York, NY|oclc=851741862}}</ref> An idempotent is said to ''split'' if it can be written as <math>f = h \circ g</math> for some f <math>g : x \to y, h : y \to x</math> with <math>g \circ h = \operatorname{id}</math>.
diff_removed:
===Idempotent functors===
A functor <math>f : C \to C</math> from a category <math>C</math> to itself is idempotent if <math>f \circ f = f</math>.<ref>Ch. I., § 5. in {{Cite book|title=Categories for the Working Mathematician|last=Mac Lane|first=Saunders|date=1978|publisher=Springer New York|isbn=1441931236|edition=Second|location=New York, NY|oclc=851741862}}</ref>

title: HD 114613
comment: /* Stellar characteristics */ clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: ically- → ically
diff_added: As HD 114613 is ending hydrogen fusion, the star must be fairly old. When combined with a spectroscopically derived mass of {{Solar mass|1.25 ± 0.03}} and a surface gravity of log 3.95 ± 0.03 g the implied age of the star is 5.20 ± 0.24 billion years,<ref name="Takeda2007"/> making it slightly older than the Sun. Though stellar metallicities typically decrease with increasing stellar age, within the age range of the [[thin disk]] a wide range of metallicities are common; HD 114613's high iron abundance of 0.19 ± 0.01 dex (155 ± 4% of the solar abundance) is therefore not unusual. The rate of giant planet occurrence for Fe/H = 0.2 dex stars is about 15%,<ref name="Mortier2013"/> which makes it fairly unsurprising that the star could host a giant planet.

In 2011, Joe Public withdrew from TT Pro League due to issues arising from owner Jack Warner <ref>{{cite web |url=https://antiguaobserver.com/pro-league-not-surprised-by-joe-public-exit/ |title=Pro League not surprised by Joe Public exit {{!}} Antigua Observer Newspaper |website=antiguaobserver.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180905142648/https://antiguaobserver.com/pro-league-not-surprised-by-joe-public-exit/ |archive-date=5 September 2018}}</ref>

title: 9 (2009 animated film)
diff_added:
9 awakens sometime after the machines have destroyed all organic life on Earth and gone dormant. He ventures outside and meets 2, an elderly inventor Stitchpunk who finds him a working voice box. The last active machine, the Cat Beast, attacks the pair, abducts 2, and takes the talisman. One-eyed healer 5 rescues 9 and takes him to [[Sanctuary]], the tower of an empty [[cathedral]] that is home to other Stitchpunks; the dogmatic leader 1, his large bodyguard 8, and the eccentric [[fountain pen]]-fingered [[clairvoyant]] 6. Against 1's demands, 9 and 5 follow the Cat Beast to a [[factory]] and rescue 2. The Cat Beast attacks the trio, but it is killed by 7, who wears a bird skull, as well as the only confirmed female of the Stitchpunks. 9 curiously connects the talisman to the dormant Fabrication Machine, accidentally reviving it; it kills 2 by sucking out his soul, and the other Stitchpunks only barely manage to escape.
7 takes 9 and 5 to an abandoned [[library]], where mute scholar twins of unknown gender 3 and 4 reveal the history of the Human-Machine war. 9 draws the symbols on the talisman, and 5 realizes the symbols match the drawings 6 is always making. 9 and 5 return to Sanctuary to investigate, but 1 reprimands them for disobeying and putting them all at risk. The Fabrication Machine begins assembling new robots; one of them, the bird-like Winged Beast, attacks the Sanctuary and burns it down. With 7's help, the Stitchpunks manage to destroy the Winged Beast using the engine of a crashed [[bomber]] and escape.
Now knowing that the talisman can be used to transfer souls, 9 reunites with the surviving Stitchpunks, who are trying to destroy the Machine. He plans to sacrifice himself to the Machine, distracting it long enough for the others to retrieve the talisman. 1 redeems himself by pushing 9 out of the way, allowing the Machine to suck out his soul instead of 9's. 9 removes the talisman and sucks the captive Stitchpunks' souls out of the Machine, destroying it. Afterward, 9 uses the talisman to release the souls of 5, 2, 8, 6 and 1. As the freed souls fly up into the sky, it starts to [[rain]]. The raindrops contain tiny flecks of bacteria, bringing organic life back into the world.

title: Greater Cairo Markets
comment: clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: between 1348–1400 → between 1348 and 1400 (2), ’s → 's
diff_added:
{{orphan|date=July 2025}}
These markets represent a vital artery of Egypt's domestic trade, attracting millions of merchants and consumers daily, and they contribute significantly to the international export flow through nearby seaports such as [[Damietta Port]], [[Alexandria Port]], [[Suez Port]], and [[Port Said Port]], as well as airports like [[Capital International Airport]] and [[Cairo International Airport]] and dry ports such as [[6th of October Dry Port]].<ref name="Egyptian12">{{cite web |title=Popular and Commercial Markets of Cairo Through the Ages |work=مصر عبر العصور |url=https://egyptian1.com/archives/5007 |accessdate=2025-07-04 |archiveurl=http://web.$archive.org/web/20231119081719/http://egyptian1.com/archives/5007 |archivedate=2023-11-19}}</ref>
'''Economic and political factors''': Decline of Mamluk capitals markets between 1348 and 1400 CE, with a temporary revival under sultans like Barsbay and Qaitbay.<ref name="Makrizi" />
'''El-Tunsi Market''' in El-Basatin: An informal market relocated to a formal organized market between 2017 and 2021.<ref name="Youm7Tunsi">{{cite web |title=Government Tackles Informal Market Fires by Building New Ones |url=https://www.youm7.com/…/3352140 |accessdate=2025-07-04}}</ref>

title: Rene di Rosa
diff_added: Rene di Rosa was born in Boston and graduated from [[Yale University]] where he was editor of the ''[[Yale Daily News]]''. He took a job in 1950 as a reporter for the ''[[San Francisco Chronicle]]'' after serving in World War II and then spending some time in Paris.<ref name="napawinelibrary1">{{cite web |url=https://www.napawinelibrary.com/nvwl-article/winter-2011-report/rene-di-rosa/ |title=René di Rosa – Napa Valley Wine Library Association |publisher=Napawinelibrary.com |date= |accessdate=2016-11-03 |archive-date=2020-03-28 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200328151616/https://www.napawinelibrary.com/nvwl-article/winter-2011-report/rene-di-rosa/ |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="sfgate1">{{cite web|last=Baker |first=Kenneth |url=http://www.sfgate.com/bayarea/article/Rene-di-Rosa-Bay-Area-art-collector-dies-3250063.php |title=Rene di Rosa, Bay Area art collector, dies |publisher=SFGate |date=2010-10-13 |accessdate=2016-11-03}}</ref> Di Rosa married artist Veronica McDonald (1934–1991)<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.click-media.net/dirosa/auction/dirosaveronica.html |title=di Rosa Preserve "Director's Cut" Art Auction |publisher=Click-media.net |date= |accessdate=2016-11-03 |archive-date=2007-08-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070819090403/http://www.click-media.net/dirosa/auction/dirosaveronica.html |url-status=dead }}</ref> in 1974.

title: Quezon, Bukidnon
diff_added: The demographic profile of Quezon reveals a total population of 114,521 as of the censal year 2024, with 56.5% concentrated in the urban barangays of Poblacion, Butong, Salawagan, San Jose, Kiburiao, Puntian, Mibantang, and Libertad. The town has a youthful population, with 62% below the age of 30, indicating a fast-growing community. Children aged 0 to 14 constitute 35% of the population, while senior citizens make up the smallest share.
